    FPS P4/P6: fail-closed repair round — purity controls quarantined, negweight-refined preflight hardened
    
    Orchestrator reconciliation + Codex fps-adopt-verifier BLOCK: both FPS unfold launchers omitted
    --bkg-mode, so the ten endpoint unfolds ran with the unfold default --bkg-mode=purity. They are
    PURITY CONTROLS, not publication inputs (selected footing = negweight-refined). No covariance/
    adoption ran. This is a repair-only round: no holder, no covariance build, no adoption, no reruns.
    
    - fps_provenance.py (NEW): ROOT-free fail-closed gate library (manifest inventory/fingerprints/
      footing; active-rollup 5-band + total identity; reported-mask binding; pure-sum-vs-subtraction
      residual; zero-block/nonzero-unified; path alias; final-adoption identity; PSD).
    - tests/test_fps_provenance.py (NEW): 24/24 ROOT-free unit tests over every failure mode.
    - fps_build_control_manifest.py (NEW) -> read-only fps_control_manifest.json (label=purity-control,
      10/10 SHA-bound, footing recovered from launcher/source; publication gate rejects it).
    - build_active_lateral_fps.py (NEW) + adopt_unified_fps.py (NEW): hardened gated producers (require
      negweight-refined manifest; mask/order/central equality; identity reconstruction). NOT run this turn.
    - p4_validate_active_lateral_fps.py / adopt_active_lateral_fps.py: hardened (5+5 inventory, mask
      binding, per-band trace, total identity, audit fingerprint; HARD pure-sum==subtraction gate,
      superseded support blocks renamed, full provenance, path-alias).
    - sbatch_unfold_active_fps.sh / run_active_fps_unfolds_interactive.sh: explicit --bkg-mode
      negweight-refined guard + separate unfolds_negweight_refined/ namespace + atomic tmp->mv +
      mode-stamped .config.json. Purity controls in unfolds/ preserved unchanged.
    
    Production remains gated on fps-adopt-verifier PASS on this patch. Residual gaps: full SHA256 of the
    ten ~74GB merged inputs deferred (bounded fingerprint + audit receipt); 285->266 mask hash deferred
    to ROOT rollup time (bound via central_cv_sha256).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

     3	chain (Agent C, P4-FPS / P6-FPS-final). ROOT-FREE by design: pure python + hashlib + numpy so
     4	every gate is unit-testable without a compute node, and the ROOT-facing scripts (rollup /
     5	validator / adopt / unified-adopt) call these same functions after loading matrices via ROOT.

     8	the unfold default --bkg-mode=purity (both launchers omit --bkg-mode), so they are PURITY CONTROLS,
     9	not publication inputs. The selected publication footing is 'negweight-refined'. These gates make
    10	that distinction structural: a purity/default/missing background mode CANNOT enter a publication
    11	rollup, and every covariance step fails closed on inventory / order / mask / footing / identity.
    12	
    13	Every `require_*` raises FpsGateError (fail-closed) with an explicit message; never returns a soft

    56	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- hashing helpers
    57	def sha256_file(path):
    58	    if not os.path.exists(path):
    59	        raise FpsGateError(f"sha256_file: missing artifact {path}")
    60	    h = hashlib.sha256()
    61	    with open(path, "rb") as f:

    71	def sha256_partial(path, edge_bytes=8 << 20):
    72	    """Bounded content fingerprint (size + head + tail) for very large files where a full-file
    73	    SHA256 is impractical (the 74GB merged endpoint inputs = 740GB of login-node I/O for all ten).
    74	    NOT a full hash: labeled 'partial-headtail' so a caller can never mistake it for one; the full

    77	    if not os.path.exists(path):

   200	    denom = max(1e-300, float(np.max(np.abs(C))))
   201	    return float(np.max(np.abs(C - C.T)) / denom)

   205	    ev = np.linalg.eigvalsh(_sym(np.asarray(C, float)))
   206	    return float(ev[0] / max(1e-300, abs(ev[-1])))

   210	    r = psd_min_eig_ratio(C)

   216	def require_square_finite(C, expected_dim=None, tag="matrix"):
   217	    C = np.asarray(C, float)

   219	        raise FpsGateError(f"{tag}: not square {C.shape}")
   220	    if expected_dim is not None and C.shape[0] != expected_dim:
   221	        raise FpsGateError(f"{tag}: dim {C.shape[0]} != expected {expected_dim}")

   238	def check_active_rollup(per_band, total, tol=1e-10):
   239	    """The active five-band lateral rollup must have EXACTLY the 5 named bands, each with a
   240	    finite nonzero-trace PSD-ish block, and total == sum(5 bands) within tol. A zero band or
   241	    zero total (the 'nothing happened' failure) raises."""

   243	    want = set(BANDS)

   245	        raise FpsGateError(
   246	            f"active rollup bands != 5 named; missing={sorted(want - got)} extra={sorted(got - want)}")

   248	    for b in BANDS:
   249	        cb = np.asarray(per_band[b], float)
   250	        require_square_finite(cb, tag=f"active band {b}")
   251	        tr = float(np.trace(cb))
   252	        if not np.isfinite(tr) or tr <= 0:
   253	            raise FpsGateError(f"active band {b}: trace {tr:.3e} <= 0 (band contributes nothing)")
   254	        acc = cb.copy() if acc is None else acc + cb
   255	    total = np.asarray(total, float)
   256	    require_square_finite(total, expected_dim=acc.shape[0], tag="active total")
   257	    if float(np.trace(total)) <= 0:
   258	        raise FpsGateError("active total: trace <= 0")
   259	    resid = float(np.max(np.abs(total - acc)))
   260	    denom = max(1e-300, float(np.max(np.abs(total))))
   261	    if resid / denom > tol:
   262	        raise FpsGateError(
   263	            f"active total != sum(5 bands): max|diff|={resid:.3e} rel={resid/denom:.2e} > {tol:.1e}")
   264	    return True

   267	def pure_sum_vs_sub_residual(C_sum, C_sub):
   268	    C_sum = np.asarray(C_sum, float); C_sub = np.asarray(C_sub, float)
   269	    if C_sum.shape != C_sub.shape:
   270	        raise FpsGateError(f"sum/sub shape mismatch {C_sum.shape} vs {C_sub.shape}")
   271	    resid = float(np.max(np.abs(C_sum - C_sub)))
   272	    denom = max(1e-300, float(np.max(np.abs(C_sum))))
   273	    return resid, resid / denom

   276	def require_pure_sum_matches_sub(C_sum, C_sub, tol=1e-9):
   277	    """The PSD-safe pure-sum swap must equal the same-source subtraction to float precision.
   278	    A large residual means the stored combined is NOT SUM(bands)+stat+ML (evidence problem),
   279	    so the pure sum cannot be trusted -> fail closed."""
   280	    resid, rel = pure_sum_vs_sub_residual(C_sum, C_sub)
   281	    if rel > tol:
   282	        raise FpsGateError(
   283	            f"pure-sum vs same-source-subtraction residual rel={rel:.3e} > {tol:.1e} "
   284	            f"(abs {resid:.3e}); stored combined is not a clean component sum")
   285	    return resid, rel

   288	def require_unified_inputs(diag_block, diag_unified, tag="unified"):
   289	    """Reject the pathological throw comparator where a block-sum variance is 0 while the unified
   290	    variance is >0 (division/inflation would be ill-defined; signals a mask/estimator mismatch)."""
   291	    db = np.asarray(diag_block, float); du = np.asarray(diag_unified, float)
   292	    if db.shape != du.shape:
   293	        raise FpsGateError(f"{tag}: block/unified diag shape mismatch {db.shape} vs {du.shape}")
   294	    bad = np.where((db <= 0) & (du > 0))[0]
   295	    if bad.size:
   296	        raise FpsGateError(
   297	            f"{tag}: {bad.size} bins have diag(C_blocksum)<=0 < diag(C_unified) "
   298	            f"(first bins {bad[:5].tolist()})")
   299	    return True

   302	def require_no_path_alias(out_path, *in_paths):
   303	    o = os.path.realpath(out_path)
   304	    for p in in_paths:
   305	        if p and os.path.realpath(p) == o:
   306	            raise FpsGateError(f"path alias: output {out_path} aliases input {p}")
   307	    return True

   309	
   310	def reconstruct_final(C_pre, C_vertical, g):
   311	    """C_final = C_pre - C_vertical + (g g^T) o C_vertical  (unified-throw inflation adoption)."""
   312	    C_pre = np.asarray(C_pre, float); C_vertical = np.asarray(C_vertical, float)
   313	    g = np.asarray(g, float)
   314	    G = np.outer(g, g)
   315	    return C_pre - C_vertical + G * C_vertical
   316

   318	def require_final_identity(C_final, C_pre, C_vertical, g, tol=1e-9, tag="final adoption"):
   319	    C_ref = _sym(reconstruct_final(C_pre, C_vertical, g))
   320	    C_fin = _sym(np.asarray(C_final, float))
   321	    resid = float(np.max(np.abs(C_fin - C_ref)))
   322	    denom = max(1e-300, float(np.max(np.abs(C_ref))))
   323	    if resid / denom > tol:
   324	        raise FpsGateError(
   325	            f"{tag}: reconstructed C_pre - C_vert + (gg^T)oC_vert != final "
   326	            f"(rel {resid/denom:.3e} > {tol:.1e})")
   327	    return resid

   330	def cov_fingerprint(C, mask_hash_val=None):
   331	    """Compact, reproducible fingerprint block for receipts."""
   332	    C = _sym(np.asarray(C, float))
   333	    ev = np.linalg.eigvalsh(C)
   334	    d = np.diag(C)
   335	    return {
   336	        "dim": int(C.shape[0]),
   337	        "rank": int(np.sum(ev > ev.max() * 1e-12)) if ev.size and ev.max() > 0 else 0,
   338	        "sqrt_trace": float(np.sqrt(max(np.trace(C), 0.0))),
   339	        "min_over_max_eig": float(ev[0] / max(1e-300, abs(ev[-1]))),
   340	        "symmetry_rel": symmetry_rel(C),
   341	        "n_pos_diag": int(np.sum(d > 0)),
   342	        "mask_hash": mask_hash_val,
   343	    }

    34	def load_flat(path):
    35	    f = ROOT.TFile.Open(path)
    36	    if not f or f.IsZombie():
    37	        raise fp.FpsGateError(f"cannot open {path}")
    38	    h = f.Get("hXSecND_flat")
    39	    if not h:
    40	        f.Close(); raise fp.FpsGateError(f"no hXSecND_flat in {path}")
    41	    a = np.array([h.GetBinContent(i + 1) for i in range(h.GetNbinsX())])
    42	    f.Close()
    43	    if a.size != fp.NBINS_EXT:
    44	        raise fp.FpsGateError(f"{path}: {a.size} bins != {fp.NBINS_EXT}")
    45	    return a
    46	
    47	
    48	def main():
    49	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    50	    ap.add_argument("--manifest", required=True, help="PUBLICATION (negweight-refined) endpoint manifest")
    51	    ap.add_argument("--cv", required=True, help="central CV unfold (defines the 285->266 reported mask)")
    52	    ap.add_argument("--out", default="active_universe_5d/fps/covariance/active_scalar_lateral_fps_cov.root")
    53	    ap.add_argument("--tol", type=float, default=1e-10)
    54	    a = ap.parse_args()

    56	    manifest = json.load(open(a.manifest))
    57	    fp.require_publication_manifest(manifest)          # inventory + fingerprints + negweight-refined
    58	    fp.require_no_path_alias(a.out, a.cv, *[e["unfold_root"] for e in manifest["endpoints"]])
    59	
    60	    # central + reported mask (CV>0), bound to the manifest's declared mask hash
    61	    if fp.sha256_file(a.cv) != manifest["central_cv_sha256"]:
    62	        raise fp.FpsGateError("CV sha256 != manifest central_cv_sha256")
    63	    cv = load_flat(a.cv)
    64	    rep = cv > 0
    65	    mh = fp.mask_hash(rep)
    66	    if manifest["reported_mask_hash"] != mh:
    67	        raise fp.FpsGateError(
    68	            f"reported-mask hash {mh} != manifest {manifest['reported_mask_hash']} "
    69	            "(central changed; covariance would be built against the wrong reporting mask)")
    70	    cv_rep = cv[rep]
    71	    n_rep = int(rep.sum())
    72	
    73	    by = {e["band"]: {} for e in manifest["endpoints"]}
    74	    for e in manifest["endpoints"]:
    75	        by[e["band"]][e["endpoint"]] = load_flat(e["unfold_root"])[rep] - cv_rep
    76	
    77	    per_band, total = {}, np.zeros((n_rep, n_rep))
    78	    for b in fp.BANDS:
    79	        if set(by[b]) != {0, 1}:
    80	            raise fp.FpsGateError(f"band {b}: endpoints != {{0,1}} (got {sorted(by[b])})")
    81	        D = np.stack([by[b][0], by[b][1]], axis=0)
    82	        Z = D - D.mean(axis=0, keepdims=True)
    83	        cov = (Z.T @ Z) / D.shape[0]              # MAT biased 1/N, mean-centered

    85	        total += cov

    90	    fo = ROOT.TFile.Open(a.out, "RECREATE")
    91	
    92	    def wcov(name, mat, title=None):
    93	        h = ROOT.TH2D(name, title or name, n_rep, 0, n_rep, n_rep, 0, n_rep)
    94	        for i in range(n_rep):

    96	                h.SetBinContent(i + 1, j + 1, float(mat[i, j]))
    97	        h.Write()
    98	
    99	    wcov("hCov_universe4d_total", total, "active selection-complete FPS lateral (sum of 5 bands)")

   101	        wcov(f"hCov_universe4d_{b}", per_band[b])

   103	        "manifest": os.path.abspath(a.manifest),
   104	        "manifest_class": fp.classify_manifest(manifest),
   105	        "reported_mask_hash": mh, "layout_fingerprint": fp.layout_fingerprint(),
   106	        "central_cv_sha256": manifest["central_cv_sha256"],
   107	        "n_reported": n_rep, "centering": "MAT biased 1/N, mean-centered", "bands": fp.BANDS,
   108	    })).Write()
   109	    fo.Close()
   110	    print(f"[rollup] wrote {a.out}  n_reported={n_rep}  "
   111	          f"sqrt_tr={np.sqrt(max(np.trace(total),0)):.4e}  mask={mh[:24]}")
   112	    for b in fp.BANDS:
   113	        print(f"   {b:22s} sqrt_tr={np.sqrt(max(np.trace(per_band[b]),0)):.4e}")

    61	def mat_gates(C, tag):
    62	    r = {}
    63	    r["shape"] = list(C.shape)
    64	    r["all_finite"] = bool(np.all(np.isfinite(C)))
    65	    asym = np.max(np.abs(C - C.T)) / max(1e-300, np.max(np.abs(C)))

    69	    r["min_eig"] = float(ev[0]); r["max_eig"] = float(ev[-1])
    70	    r["min_over_max_eig"] = float(ev[0] / max(1e-300, ev[-1]))
    71	    r["psd"] = bool(ev[0] >= -1e-12 * abs(ev[-1]))
    72	    d = np.diag(C)
    73	    r["diag_finite_nonneg"] = bool(np.all(np.isfinite(d)) and np.all(d >= -1e-30))
    74	    r["sqrt_trace"] = float(np.sqrt(np.clip(np.trace(Cs), 0, None)))
    75	    r["n_reported"] = int(np.sum(d > 0))

    77	          f"asym={asym:.2e} min/max_eig={r['min_over_max_eig']:.2e} PSD={r['psd']} "
    78	          f"sqrt_tr={r['sqrt_trace']:.4e}")
    79	    return r

     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""Completeness gate for FPS active-endpoint unfolds (Agent C). A wall-killed unfold leaves a
     3	present-but-TRUNCATED .root (ROOT auto-recovered on reopen, or the final hXSecND_flat/histograms
     4	never written) -- the exact failure that makes a naive `-s`/existence skip consume a bad file. This
     5	validates each output is COMPLETE so the driver never skips (and the P4 chain never ingests) a
     6	truncated unfold. Exit 0 iff EVERY requested file is complete.
     7	
     8	  fps_unfold_complete.py f1.root [f2 ...]   # explicit files
     9	  fps_unfold_complete.py --all              # the 10 canonical endpoints (relative to CWD=nd-unfolding)
    10	
    11	COMPLETE := TFile opens & not zombie; NOT TFile::kRecovered (recovery => truncated write);
    12	hXSecND_flat present with nbins==285 (FPS 15pt x 19pz extended grid), all bins finite, sum>0;
    13	globalCompleteness TParameter present, finite, > MIN_COMPLETE (the unfold's '[CHECK] c=' value).
    14	"""
    15	import argparse, os, sys
    16	import numpy as np
    17	import ROOT
    18	
    19	ROOT.gROOT.SetBatch(True)
    20	BANDS = ["BeamAngleX", "BeamAngleY", "MuonResolution",
    21	         "Muon_Energy_MINERvA", "Muon_Energy_MINOS"]
    22	OUTDIR = "active_universe_5d/fps/unfolds"
    23	NAME = "fps2d_xsec_MEFHC_5iter_lgbm_uni_full_{b}_{ep}.root"
    24	EXPECT_NBINS = 285          # 15 (pt) x 19 (pz) FPS extended grid
    25	MIN_COMPLETE = 0.50         # sanity floor; healthy unfolds report c=1.0000
    26	
    27	
    28	def check(path):
    29	    r = {"path": os.path.basename(path), "ok": False, "why": ""}
    30	    if not os.path.exists(path):
    31	        r["why"] = "missing"; return r
    32	    if os.path.getsize(path) < 1024:
    33	        r["why"] = f"tiny ({os.path.getsize(path)}B)"; return r
    34	    f = ROOT.TFile.Open(path)
    35	    if not f or f.IsZombie():
    36	        r["why"] = "zombie/unopenable"; return r
    37	    if f.TestBit(ROOT.TFile.kRecovered):
    38	        f.Close(); r["why"] = "kRecovered (truncated/uncleanly-closed write)"; return r
    39	    h = f.Get("hXSecND_flat")
    40	    if not h:
    41	        f.Close(); r["why"] = "no hXSecND_flat"; return r
    42	    nb = h.GetNbinsX()
    43	    if nb != EXPECT_NBINS:
    44	        f.Close(); r["why"] = f"nbins {nb} != {EXPECT_NBINS}"; return r
    45	    vals = np.array([h.GetBinContent(i + 1) for i in range(nb)])
    46	    if not np.all(np.isfinite(vals)):
    47	        f.Close(); r["why"] = "non-finite bins in hXSecND_flat"; return r
    48	    s = float(vals.sum())
    49	    if s <= 0:
    50	        f.Close(); r["why"] = f"sum<=0 ({s:.3e})"; return r
    51	    gc = f.Get("globalCompleteness")
    52	    gcv = float(gc.GetVal()) if gc else None
    53	    f.Close()
    54	    if gcv is None or not np.isfinite(gcv):
    55	        r["why"] = "no/NaN globalCompleteness"; return r
    56	    if gcv < MIN_COMPLETE:
    57	        r["why"] = f"globalCompleteness {gcv:.4f} < {MIN_COMPLETE}"; return r
    58	    r.update(ok=True, sum=s, gc=gcv, nbins=nb); return r
    59	
    60	
    61	def main():
    62	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    63	    ap.add_argument("files", nargs="*")
    64	    ap.add_argument("--all", action="store_true")
    65	    a = ap.parse_args()
    66	    files = list(a.files)
    67	    if a.all:
    68	        files = [os.path.join(OUTDIR, NAME.format(b=b, ep=ep)) for b in BANDS for ep in (0, 1)]
    69	    if not files:
    70	        sys.exit("no files given")
    71	    nbad = 0
    72	    for p in files:
    73	        r = check(p)
    74	        if r["ok"]:
    75	            print(f"[OK]   {r['path']} nbins={r['nbins']} sum={r['sum']:.4e} gc={r['gc']:.4f}")
    76	        else:
    77	            nbad += 1; print(f"[BAD]  {r['path']} :: {r['why']}")
    78	    print(f"\n{len(files) - nbad}/{len(files)} complete")
    79	    sys.exit(0 if nbad == 0 else 1)
    80	
    81	
    82	if __name__ == "__main__":
    83	    main()

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""Blocker 7 (Agent C): ROOT-free unit tests for the FPS provenance/gate library. Exercises every
     3	fail-closed path the covariance chain depends on: manifest inventory (wrong/missing/extra/dup),
     4	mismatched layout/mask/central/bkg-mode footing, active-rollup band inventory + zero band/total,
     5	pure-sum-vs-subtraction residual, zero-block/nonzero-unified, path aliasing, and the final
     6	adoption identity reconstruction. No ROOT, no compute node: pure numpy + dict fixtures.
     7	
     8	  python -m pytest tests/test_fps_provenance.py     # or:  python tests/test_fps_provenance.py
     9	"""
    10	import os
    11	import sys
    12	
    13	import numpy as np
    14	
    15	sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), ".."))
    16	import fps_provenance as fp
    17	
    18	
    19	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- fixtures / helpers
    20	def _spd(n, seed):
    21	    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
    22	    A = rng.standard_normal((n, n))
    23	    return A @ A.T / n + np.eye(n) * 1e-6
    24	
    25	
    26	def make_manifest(bkg_mode=fp.PUBLICATION_BKG_MODE, drop=None, extra=None, dup=None,
    27	                  bad_layout=False, mixed_mode=False, bad_footing=None):
    28	    lay = "BAD" if bad_layout else fp.layout_fingerprint()
    29	    mh, ch = "maskhash-xyz", "central-xyz"
    30	    eps = []
    31	    pairs = [(b, ep) for b in fp.BANDS for ep in fp.ENDPOINTS]
    32	    if drop:
    33	        pairs = [p for p in pairs if p != drop]
    34	    if extra:
    35	        pairs = pairs + [extra]
    36	    if dup:
    37	        pairs = pairs + [dup]
    38	    for i, (b, ep) in enumerate(pairs):
    39	        foot = dict(fp.REQUIRED_FOOTING)
    40	        foot["bkg_mode"] = bkg_mode
    41	        if mixed_mode and i == 0:
    42	            foot["bkg_mode"] = fp.CONTROL_BKG_MODE
    43	        if bad_footing and (b, ep) == bad_footing[0]:
    44	            foot[bad_footing[1]] = bad_footing[2]
    45	        eps.append({"band": b, "endpoint": ep,
    46	                    "layout_fingerprint": lay, "reported_mask_hash": mh, "central_hash": ch,
    47	                    "footing": foot})
    48	    return {"layout_fingerprint": lay, "reported_mask_hash": mh, "central_hash": ch,
    49	            "endpoints": eps}
    50	
    51	
    52	def _raises(fn):
    53	    try:
    54	        fn()
    55	    except fp.FpsGateError:
    56	        return True
    57	    return False
    58

    60	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- manifest inventory
    61	def test_manifest_ok():
    62	    assert fp.require_publication_manifest(make_manifest())
    63	
    64	
    65	def test_manifest_missing_endpoint():
    66	    assert _raises(lambda: fp.require_manifest_inventory(make_manifest(drop=("MuonResolution", 1))))
    67	
    68	
    69	def test_manifest_extra_endpoint():
    70	    assert _raises(lambda: fp.require_manifest_inventory(make_manifest(extra=("Bogus", 0))))
    71	
    72	
    73	def test_manifest_duplicate_endpoint():
    74	    assert _raises(lambda: fp.require_manifest_inventory(make_manifest(dup=("BeamAngleX", 0))))
    75	
    76	
    77	def test_manifest_bad_layout():
    78	    assert _raises(lambda: fp.require_common_fingerprints(make_manifest(bad_layout=True)))
    79	
    80

    82	    # purity controls must be rejected by the publication gate
    83	    assert _raises(lambda: fp.require_publication_manifest(make_manifest(bkg_mode=fp.CONTROL_BKG_MODE)))
    84

    86	def test_footing_missing_token():
    87	    m = make_manifest(bad_footing=(("BeamAngleY", 0), "seed", 999))
    88	    assert _raises(lambda: fp.require_footing(m))

    90	
    91	def test_classify_mixed_fails():
    92	    assert _raises(lambda: fp.classify_manifest(make_manifest(mixed_mode=True)))
    93	
    94	
    95	def test_classify_control_and_publication():
    96	    assert fp.classify_manifest(make_manifest(bkg_mode=fp.CONTROL_BKG_MODE)) == "purity-control"
    97	    assert fp.classify_manifest(make_manifest(bkg_mode=fp.PUBLICATION_BKG_MODE)) == "publication"

   100	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- active rollup
   101	def test_active_rollup_ok():
   102	    per = {b: _spd(6, i) for i, b in enumerate(fp.BANDS)}
   103	    tot = sum(per.values())

   157	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- final identity
   158	def test_final_identity_ok():
   159	    C_pre = _spd(10, 2); C_vert = _spd(10, 3) * 0.3
   160	    g = np.linspace(1.0, 1.5, 10)
   161	    C_final = fp.reconstruct_final(C_pre, C_vert, g)
   162	    assert fp.require_final_identity(C_final, C_pre, C_vert, g) < 1e-6
   163	
   164	
   165	def test_final_identity_violation():
   166	    C_pre = _spd(10, 2); C_vert = _spd(10, 3) * 0.3
   167	    g = np.linspace(1.0, 1.5, 10)
   168	    C_final = fp.reconstruct_final(C_pre, C_vert, g) + 1e-2 * np.max(np.abs(C_pre))
   169	    assert _raises(lambda: fp.require_final_identity(C_final, C_pre, C_vert, g))
   170	
   171	
   172	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- matrix gates
   173	def test_reported_cov_mask_binding():
   174	    C = _spd(266, 5)
   175	    assert fp.require_reported_cov(C, 266, "mhX", "mhX")

### Blocking defects

1. **Publication manifests are not artifact-bound.**

   `require_publication_manifest()` validates selected strings and inventory but does not require the publication label, schema, endpoint/input/config hashes, audit binding, or non-deferred mask evidence: [fps_provenance.py:136](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/fps_provenance.py:136), [fps_provenance.py:183](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/fps_provenance.py:183).

   The active builder opens endpoint paths without verifying their SHA256, input hash, config receipt, source hash, axes, or flat/TH2 C-order relationship: [build_active_lateral_fps.py:73](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/build_active_lateral_fps.py:73).

   Direct negative probes showed the validator accepts a manifest labeled `purity-control`, a manifest with no artifact paths/hashes, a deferred mask, and missing endpoint fingerprints. The tests’ nominal manifest fixture itself contains no artifact hashes: [test_fps_provenance.py:45](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/tests/test_fps_provenance.py:45).

2. **Purity rejection is not enforced by every rollup/adoption path.**

   P4’s manifest is optional; absence is recorded but not made a validation failure: [p4_validate_active_lateral_fps.py:88](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/p4_validate_active_lateral_fps.py:88), [p4_validate_active_lateral_fps.py:108](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/p4_validate_active_lateral_fps.py:108).

   Neither adoption script requires the publication manifest or a hash-bound P4 receipt: [adopt_active_lateral_fps.py:65](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/adopt_active_lateral_fps.py:65), [adopt_unified_fps.py:56](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/adopt_unified_fps.py:56). Consequently, purity-derived inputs could still be supplied directly.

   Durable instructions also retain an unsafe resume path through the old purity namespace and generic analyzer: [FPS_UQ_CORRECTED_STATE.md:291](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/uq_fps/corrected/FPS_UQ_CORRECTED_STATE.md:291).

3. **The launchers are mode-explicit but not transactionally fail-safe.**

   The batch launcher correctly enforces the mode and separate namespace, but skips any nonempty existing output without provenance validation: [sbatch_unfold_active_fps.sh:34](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/sbatch_unfold_active_fps.sh:34), [sbatch_unfold_active_fps.sh:39](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/sbatch_unfold_active_fps.sh:39), [sbatch_unfold_active_fps.sh:41](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/sbatch_unfold_active_fps.sh:41). Its config sidecar is written non-atomically after the ROOT rename and lacks output/input hashes and central/mask/source binding: [sbatch_unfold_active_fps.sh:53](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/sbatch_unfold_active_fps.sh:53).

   The interactive launcher swallows worker failures with `|| echo`, so it can exit successfully after failed endpoints: [run_active_fps_unfolds_interactive.sh:43](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/run_active_fps_unfolds_interactive.sh:43). It can also create receipts for stale pre-existing outputs: [run_active_fps_unfolds_interactive.sh:60](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/run_active_fps_unfolds_interactive.sh:60).

4. **Both declared residual gaps are blockers before endpoint production.**

   Merged inputs are bound only by file size plus 8 MiB head/tail hashes: [fps_provenance.py:71](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/fps_provenance.py:71). That cannot detect changes in most of a roughly 74-GB file.

   The exact reported-mask field remains explicitly deferred: [fps_control_manifest.json:51](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/active_universe_5d/fps/covariance/fps_control_manifest.json:51). Since the exact mask is already reproducible from the existing central ROOT, deferring it until rollup unnecessarily permits endpoints to run against an unbound layout.

5. **Unified provenance and mean-shift preservation remain optional.**

   `hJointMeanShift` is copied only if present rather than required, dimension-checked, finite-checked, and provenance-bound: [adopt_unified_fps.py:128](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/adopt_unified_fps.py:128). The unified path does not establish exact central/mask/order fingerprint equality across all inputs.

6. **The repository commit gate is unmet.**

   The commit adds campaign launchers but does not update `VALIDATION_LEDGER.md`, `ND_OMNIFOLD_RUN_LOG.md`, and `ND_OMNIFOLD_STATUS.md`, contrary to the repository’s same-commit gate: [AGENTS.md:40](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/AGENTS.md:40). The corrected FPS state repeats that requirement: [FPS_UQ_CORRECTED_STATE.md:150](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/uq_fps/corrected/FPS_UQ_CORRECTED_STATE.md:150).

### Smallest required repairs

- Commit exact full-file SHA256 or full-content cryptographic Merkle fingerprints for all ten merged inputs, plus the exact 266/285 mask fingerprint, before running endpoints.
- Add a publication-manifest builder that binds each of the ten endpoint ROOTs to its full input hash, output hash, atomic config receipt, launcher/source hashes, exact configuration, central hash, mask, axes and C-order.
- Make that manifest mandatory and hash-verified in P4, active adoption and unified adoption; require a hash-bound PASS receipt at each downstream transition.
- Make endpoint publication atomic, with the receipt published last. Skip only when both ROOT and receipt fully validate. Propagate every interactive worker failure as a nonzero launcher result.
- Require and validate the joint mean shift and centering policy; verify central/mask/order fingerprints across unified inputs.
- Remove the stale purity resume instructions and update the ledger, ND run log and ND status in the repair commit.
- Extend tests with integration-level negatives for missing/deferred hashes, purity labels, stale outputs, endpoint/config hash mismatches, absent mean shift, worker failure and adoption without a validated manifest.

No endpoint, covariance, or adoption run is authorized by this verdict.
